vulnerability.reference: Practitioner ignorance of safety null-space fragility during standard fine-tuning workflows.
vulnerability.generated: Existing fine-tuning defenses lack mechanisms to prevent reversal or misapplication of the added safety delta, leaving safety constraints brittle.

impact.reference: Domain-specific models in medicine, law, or finance output dangerous advice under fine-tuning drift.
impact.generated: Fine-tuned LLMs generate unsafe outputs that harm end users and break safety alignment guarantees.

dose_response.reference: Fine-tuning shifts weights from safety null space, which in domain-specific adaptation exposes high-stakes outputs to dangerous advice and liability.
dose_response.generated: Safety vector addition for realignment introduces a reversible delta that, when fine-tuning practitioners apply it, triggers the absence of defenses preventing delta reversal, leading to unsafe outputs that harm end users and break alignment guarantees.
